title: Postmortem - Incident #6023: Cascading Failure across Order and Inventory Services
content:
Postmortem - Incident #6023: Cascading Failure across Order and Inventory Services

### Incident Summary
On May 10, multiple alerts fired simultaneously for order-service, inventory-service, and db-service. Request queue depths filled up rapidly, causing an outage across all purchasing flows.

### Root Cause
The db-service database server lock contention occurred due to a long-running reporting query. This caused db-service responses to inventory-service to slow down. Inventory-service threads blocked waiting for db-service, which in turn blocked order-service. The system collapsed due to cascading timeout propagation.

### Resolution
Rather than executing a shotgun restart of all services (which would fail again instantly), the database administrator terminated the long-running reporting query on db-service. Once database locks were released, downstream services recovered automatically within 5 minutes.

### Action Items
- Separate transactional and analytical/reporting databases.
- Enforce strict gRPC timeouts of 2 seconds between microservices.
